intent:
PREMISE CONTRACT (story 18)
  one_sentence_event:    The narrator leaves the entryway alone in the morning, walks to the station, finds the friend already waiting, and the two of them board a train together — converting yesterday's just-stepped-outside ending into today's actual journey.
  change_of_state:       at s0 the narrator is solitary, just-shod, just-outside the entryway and stationary; at the closer two people are inside a moving train carrying them away from the station. X (alone, on foot, near home) ≠ Y (paired, riding, leaving the station behind).
  scene_novelty_claim:   I am NOT writing a bookstore / entryway / home_morning story. Scene is the train station (駅) at morning — a transit-and-departure scene, not the indoor/threshold scenes of stories 15–17.
  anchor_novelty_claim:  Anchor is 電車 (train). Last appeared in story 5 (last_use 5) as background; never used as anchor before. Not in the last-5-anchor block list (ノート, 傘, 手紙, 窓, 靴).
  closer_shape_claim:    Closer shape: action — `noun-WA + adverb-NI + noun-KARA + verb`. Train-as-subject leaves the station. NOT a noun-WO-noun line (st 15), NOT a noun-DE-noun-WO-noun-noun-WO-noun (st 16), NOT a noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-WO-noun-noun-E-noun co-action line (st 17).
  why_not_filler:        s0 EARNS its slot by absorbing all three R1 reinforcements (玄関, 履く, 外) into a single narrative beat — the morning departure — rather than three bolted-on lines. s1 EARNS its slot as the transit between home and the station (geographically necessary, sets up the encounter). s2 EARNS the friend's entrance via her active waiting (友達が私を待っていました) — she is not teleported. s3 EARNS the counter (二人で) by stating the shared act of waiting that motivates the dialogue. s4 EARNS the dialogue + N5_masho reinforcement via the friend's natural invitation to board. s5 EARNS the boarding action, paying off the wait. s6 closer EARNS by giving the train its own subject-verb beat: it carries them away.
  obligations_absorbed:  must-introduce N5_counters absorbed at s3 via 二人 (paradigm anchor). must-reinforce N5_masho absorbed at s4 via 「電車に乗りましょう」. R1 reinforcements (story 17's mints): 玄関 (W00102) at s0, 履く (W00104) at s0, 外 (W00103) at s0 — all woven into the single departure beat.

FORBIDDEN THIS STORY:
  scene_class    (last 3 stories): bookstore, entryway, home_morning
  anchor_object  (last 5 stories): ノート, 傘, 手紙, 窓, 靴
  closer_shape   (last 3 stories): noun-DE-noun-WO-noun-noun-WO-noun, noun-TO-noun-WA-noun-WO-noun-noun-E-noun, noun-WO-noun
  opening_seqs   (last 3 stories' first 3 content tokens):
    - 冷たい / 朝
    - 道 / 小さい / 本屋
    - 今日 / 私 / 家

sentences.jp: 次の朝、玄関で靴を履いて、外へ出ました。
sentences.en: The next morning, I put on my shoes at the entryway and stepped outside.
I walked all the way to the station.

sentences.jp: 駅で友達が私を待っていました。
sentences.en: At the station, my friend was waiting for me.

sentences.jp: 二人で電車を待ちました。
sentences.en: The two of us waited for the train.

sentences.jp: 友達は「電車に乗りましょう」と言いました。
sentences.en: My friend said, "Let's get on the train."

sentences.jp: 電車が来て、二人で乗りました。
sentences.en: The train came, and the two of us boarded.

sentences.jp: 電車が静かに駅から出ました。
sentences.en: The train quietly pulled out of the station.
